mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (for Physical Layer functions: Pulsing, Data encoding, Negotiation, Transmission, Signaling, Physical attachment, Attenuation handling)
keyPoints: The Physical Layer is responsible for the transmission and reception of raw bit streams over a physical medium.
It defines the electrical, mechanical, procedural, and functional specifications for activating, maintaining, and deactivating the physical link between devices.
Key functions include data encoding, signal transmission, physical topology, and defining hardware specifications like cables, connectors, and NICs.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Physical Layer (Layer 1) with the Data Link Layer (Layer 2) and its framing responsibilities.
Assuming all physical media (e.g., copper, fiber, wireless) use the same signaling methods and have identical characteristics like bandwidth and attenuation.
